conversations.content: Tool
Ok what problem is your tool supposed to solve?
Generates logo variations from a text brand description.
And who exactly is the target user for this?
Small business owners who aren't very technical.
And how does it actually solve that problem, like what's the mechanism?
It watches what the user does and learns their patterns over time.
Does it use AI or is it more rule-based / manual?
AI for the core logic, but the UI is pretty simple and deterministic.
What does the user interaction look like — is there a UI, a CLI, something else?
Clean web UI — nothing fancy, just works.

So what you're building is basically a tool that generates logo variations from a text brand description — and the user interacts with it through a clean interface, setting their preferences so the AI can do the heavy lifting for them?
Yes exactly.
App
Ok what problem is your app supposed to solve?
So like anonymous Q&A platform for asking career advice from seniors — that's the whole thing.
And how does it actually solve that problem, like what's the mechanism?
It watches what the user does and learns their patterns over time.
Does it use AI or is it more rule-based / manual?
It uses AI but also lets users override with manual filters if they want.

So what you're building is basically a app that anonymous Q&A platform for asking career advice from seniors — and the user interacts with it through a clean interface, setting their preferences so the AI can do the heavy lifting for them?
Yeah that's it.
App
Ok what problem is your app supposed to solve?
So like async video review tool for design feedback like Loom meets Figma — that's the whole thing.
Does it require user accounts or can it work without auth?
Can work without auth for the basic stuff, but accounts unlock more features.
What does the user interaction look like — is there a UI, a CLI, something else?
Mobile app with a simple home screen.
What makes this different from existing tools out there?
It's way simpler — most alternatives are bloated with features nobody uses.
Where does the data come from? Does the user input it, or does it pull from somewhere?
It reads from the user's existing files or accounts with permission.
What would the simplest MVP version look like?
A simple web page where you paste your input and get a result back.
Have you thought about how it would be monetized or distributed?
Honestly haven't thought that far yet, just want to build it first.
And who exactly is the target user for this?
Developers and tech-savvy people mainly.

So what you're building is basically a app that async video review tool for design feedback like Loom meets Figma — and the user interacts with it through a clean interface, setting their preferences so the AI can do the heavy lifting for them?
Yes exactly.
Chrome extension
Ok what problem is your chrome extension supposed to solve?
Basically checks if a product has ethical sourcing certifications.
And who exactly is the target user for this?
Power users who want more control over their tools.
And how does it actually solve that problem, like what's the mechanism?
It scrapes data from multiple sources and presents it in one place.
Does it use AI or is it more rule-based / manual?
Uses AI, yeah — specifically an LLM to understand natural language inputs.
What does the user interaction look like — is there a UI, a CLI, something else?
Just a popup — minimal UI, like a floating panel.

So what you're building is basically a chrome extension that checks if a product has ethical sourcing certifications — and the user interacts with it through a clean interface, setting their preferences so the AI can do the heavy lifting for them?
Yep, that's the whole thing.
Chrome extension

And how does it actually solve that problem, like what's the mechanism?
It integrates with existing tools via APIs and adds a layer of intelligence on top.

Fully AI-driven.

That's a good way to put it, yes.
App
Ok what problem is your app supposed to solve?
Basically local skill-swap platform — teach what you know, learn what you don't.
Have you thought about how it would be monetized or distributed?
Free tier with a paid plan for heavy usage — classic freemium.
Is this single-player or does it have any collaborative features?
Solo tool, collaboration doesn't really fit the use case.

Combination — some user-provided, some fetched in real time.
What would the simplest MVP version look like?
A working CLI tool that handles the main use case end to end.
Does it require user accounts or can it work without auth?
No auth needed — it's fully local.

The AI understands nuance, not just keywords.
And who exactly is the target user for this?
Indie hackers and solo founders.

That's a good way to put it, yes.
